summary: Comprehensive unit test suite with good coverage of core requirements (classification, grouping, formatting, validation, exit codes, CLI). All major code paths exercised. Well-organized with clear fixtures and pytest idioms. Edge cases tested include boundary conditions (exact warning edge), empty groups, missing files, invalid JSON, and incomplete secrets. Minor gaps: no explicit tests for zero/negative warning_days, date.today() default behavior, or required_by as empty list. JSON output validation could be deeper. No parametrization, which increases code duplication across similar test cases. Complemented by strong end-to-end act-based tests. Would reliably catch most regressions but misses some obscure scenarios.
